phase1.output:
=== File I/O Basics — fstream ===

--- Writing to file ---
✅ File written successfully!

--- Reading from file ---
Read: Hero 100 20 15
Read: Aragorn 120 25 18
Read: Gandalf 80 15 10

--- Appending to file ---
✅ Appended successfully!

--- Reading with formatted input ---
Players loaded:
  Hero (HP: 100, ATK: 20, DEF: 15)
  Aragorn (HP: 120, ATK: 25, DEF: 18)
  Gandalf (HP: 80, ATK: 15, DEF: 10)
  Legolas (HP: 90, ATK: 18, DEF: 12)

--- File Modes ---
std::ios::out  — write (overwrites)
std::ios::in   — read
std::ios::app  — append
std::ios::ate  — append to end
std::ios::trunc — truncate (default for out)

--- File Status ---
❌ File does not exist!

--- File I/O Methods ---
✅ ofstream — output (write)
✅ ifstream — input (read)
✅ fstream — both
phase1.explanation:
🎮 **File I/O saves and loads game data.**

**File stream classes:**

| Class | Use |
|-------|-----|
| `std::ofstream` | Write to file |
| `std::ifstream` | Read from file |
| `std::fstream` | Read and write |

**Opening files:**
```cpp
std::ofstream out("file.txt");           // Write (overwrite)
std::ifstream in("file.txt");            // Read
std::ofstream append("file.txt", std::ios::app);  // Append
```

**File modes:**

| Mode | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `std::ios::out` | Write (overwrite) |
| `std::ios::in` | Read |
| `std::ios::app` | Append |
| `std::ios::ate` | Append to end |

**Reading methods:**

| Method | Use |
|--------|-----|
| `>>` | Formatted input |
| `getline()` | Read line by line |
| `get()` | Read character by character |

**Game uses:**

| Use | Why |
|-----|-----|
| **Save games** | Save player progress |
| **Load games** | Load saved progress |
| **Configuration** | Load game settings |
| **High scores** | Save leaderboard |

**PlusPlus-chan says:**
"File I/O makes your game persistent. Save games, load saves."
phase1.openingDialogue: fstream: save and load your game. 💾

phase2.errorMessage: (No error — but the data might not be written! Silent failure.)

phase3.prompt:
Write a C++ program that:

1. Creates a file called "inventory.txt" and writes:
   - "Sword 50 3"
   - "Shield 40 5"
   - "Potion 25 1"
   (name value weight)

2. Reads the file back and prints each item

3. Appends a new item: "Bow 35 2"

4. Reads the file again and prints all items

5. Checks if the file exists before reading

Requirements:
- Use `#include <fstream>`
- Use `ofstream` and `ifstream`
- Check if files are open
dialogueHints: Read: `std::ifstream in("inventory.txt"); while (in >> name >> value >> weight)`
phase3.openingDialogue: fstream: save and load your game. 💾
